description: Lock advanced_optics_space_based to early spaceborne optical instruments anchored to TIROS-1, not generic adaptive optics, laser systems, or digital image-processing workflows.

checks.reason: The corrected scope is anchored to TIROS-1's April 1960 orbital optical imaging mission.
A spaceborne optical payload requires a spacecraft or satellite platform.
The TIROS anchor used television pictures and recorded images, making imaging lineage enabling but not the only form of later optical payload.
Telescope lineage belongs to the orbital-observatory branch without forcing every spacecraft optical instrument to be a telescope.
Laser systems are later or specialized payloads, not required by the 1960 TIROS optical-imaging anchor.
DSP is downstream analysis context for later remote sensing and astronomy, not a direct prerequisite for the first orbital TV imaging payload.
